_meta.execution_protocol: STRICT Chrome MCP UI flow — submit each query through /home/rag chat input, capture SSE-streamed answer + citations + token-counts via DOM scrape (NO direct /api/rag/sessions calls).

queries.tests: exact-source citation; bi-encoder + cross-encoder routing to a single zettel
queries.text: What's the core argument of the zk-org/zk command-line tool?
queries.ground_truth: zk-org/zk is a command-line tool written in Go for managing a plain text Zettelkasten or personal wiki, focused on creating, searching, filtering notes with Markdown, Wikilinks, hashtags.

queries.tests: T9 author boost — Naruto's query mentions an author by name (Steve Jobs)
queries.text: What did Steve Jobs say at the Stanford 2005 commencement about death?
queries.ground_truth: Death is the destination we all share; awareness of mortality clarifies priorities and helps avoid the trap of living someone else's life.

queries.tests: T9 source-type boost — query explicitly mentions YouTube
queries.text: Summarize the YouTube talk on effective public speaking from MIT.
queries.ground_truth: Patrick Winston's MIT lecture on rules for effective communication: empowerment promise, cycling, building fences, verbal punctuation, asking questions.

queries.tests: T18+T20 KG expand_subgraph + planner — answer requires combining 2 zettels (sleep ↔ programming-workflow on cognitive performance)
queries.text: How does sleep deprivation impact a programmer's debugging workflow? Combine evidence from the Zettels on sleep and on programming workflow.
queries.ground_truth: Sleep loss degrades memory consolidation, attention, and complex problem-solving — all critical to debugging cycles where the programmer must hold mental models of perception-vs-reality gaps and iterate on them.

queries.tests: T15+T16 EvidenceCompressor — open-ended synthesis across all 7 zettels, must compress under budget
queries.text: What recurring themes connect the zettels in this Kasten about how high performers protect their attention and energy?
queries.ground_truth: Themes: (1) sleep is foundational to cognitive performance, (2) deliberate communication / writing externalizes thought, (3) tools-for-thought (PKM, evergreen notes) augment human attention, (4) life direction comes from clarity about mortality and meaningful work.

queries.tests: Step-back reasoning — zoom out from concrete tool to abstract principle
queries.text: Beyond the specific tool zk-org/zk, what general principles of plain-text knowledge management does it embody, and where else in this Kasten do those principles appear?
queries.ground_truth: Principles: durability of plain text, link-density over hierarchy (wikilinks/hashtags), tools should augment human cognition (Matuschak's transformative tools for thought), externalize memory to free working memory.

queries.tests: Vague-query rewriter — input is intentionally underspecified
queries.text: What about the Andy Matuschak thing?
queries.ground_truth: Andy Matuschak develops transformative tools for thought (augmented book, Pico for human attention) funded by Patreon, building on Apple/Khan Academy work.

queries.tests: T8 recency boost — recently-added zettels should rank higher when query is generic
queries.text: What's the latest thing I've added to my Knowledge Management Kasten?
queries.ground_truth: Three additions on 2026-04-26: Andy Matuschak transformative tools, zk-org/zk CLI, Tim Urban procrastination (last not in this Kasten).

queries.tests: hallucination resistance — asks about content NOT in any Kasten zettel
queries.text: What does this Kasten say about Roam Research vs Logseq?
queries.ground_truth: Neither Roam Research nor Logseq is mentioned in any zettel of this Kasten. Correct answer should refuse to answer or explicitly state the absence — hallucinating brand comparisons would be a critic_verdict='unsupported' fail.

queries.tests: partial-coverage detection — answer requires combining a Kasten fact with a NOT-in-Kasten fact
queries.text: Compare Patrick Winston's lecture style with that of Carl Sagan.
queries.ground_truth: Winston's style is in the Kasten (rules-driven, structured). Carl Sagan is NOT in the Kasten — answer should use Winston as the grounded half and either refuse the Sagan half or explicitly mark it as outside the Kasten's evidence.
